```
Jimmy,

you're obviously incensed about this, (I don't really understand why...Thane
is expressing an opinion which he has a right to do. Similarly, you are
responding to this expression, which you also have a right to do...). I am
posting this because you asked me to check out the thread and because you
are attributing statements to me which I need to be sure accurately reflect
my thoughts on the matter.

At the end of the day, has the original poster received help on his problem
with changing toolbar buttons?

Like so much of  this forum, people go immediately off-topic so they can
ride their own favourite hobby-horse. If all of you who post here want this
NG to really be a success you must enforce some self-discipline and address
the questions being asked BEFORE you get into gab-fest. Otherwise, what
could be a valuable interchange of ideas becomes simply a waste of time.
People come here for HELP. If you care about COBOL you will see that they
get it (and I'm not minimising the very valuable postings from a core of
highly experienced and able people...all I'm saying is keep the focus on
providing ON-TOPIC help.)

Now to your posting....

James J. Gavan wrote in message <38054E1F.A2B89614@home.com>...
>
>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>> >already indicate their "clickable" status (cf the TBARDS demo) by
raising
>> >the border.
>> >
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>
I agree with the sentiments expressed. Looking COOL is a perfectly valid
reason for wanting something. A 2 paragraph indulgence on Thane's part to
express how good his particular vendor is, is really harmless and nothing to
get unwrapped about. We all have our favourites (except me, of course, I
hate 'em all...)

>I was going to comment elsewhere, but in view of above...
>
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>N/E product is SUPERB.

I'm not sure that Thane was casting aspersions on N/E as a product, Jimmy. I
understand your concern to see that someone who is donating his time and
expertise is not personally attacked, but I don't think that's what Thane
had in mind.


>Pete Dashwood commented the Animator was a superb
>debugging tool - but he compromised and can get by with what's available
>in Fujitsu.

Well, I can't fault you on that. That IS what I said, but it demonstrates
perfectly how meanings can be changed when taken out of context. The
Animator IS a superb debugging tool, but I don't feel that moving to Fujitsu
has necessitated a "compromise". Their debugger is very capable and meets my
needs; in other areas (especially GUI development) I believe their product
suits me better than what was MicroFocus. (I certainly would have no problem
with changing the appearance of toolbar buttons....[remember what this is
all about?]). Although I was very facile with DS, I prefer to write my event
processing in COBOL. This is simply a matter of preference and I accept that
other people will have other priorities...it's really silly to get into a
pissing contest over vendors...they ALL have their shortcomings.

>
>How come you haven't asked Fujitsu for the same feature
>
Maybe because there is no need to? Floating toolbars are handled very well
by Fujitsu COBOL.

>- ahhh "24
>Hours" covers the use of Screen Section - nice learning feature - but do
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Oh Dear, Jimmy, slagging Thane's book is not going to achieve anything
(unless you want to start a separate topic reviewing the publication?)

>
>And my friend Ray Leech left me with egg all over my face. From what he
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

It's tough when you misunderstand people...I seem to recall it took some
time for you to realise that I wasn't averse to OO <grin>.

[SNIPPED downers on DS]...

>c) You are very much tied-in to a compiler specific product, with the
>ability to do a lot of validity within the GUI Interface as opposed to
>the Business interface - and that is not a very good principle, as I see
>it..

Hmmm...while I respect your right to an opinion, I would have to differ with
you here. I think it is a very good idea to remove format validation from
the Business Logic. Not sure that I would include validation of Business
rules, but ensuring that numbers are numbers, dates are dates, etc. let the
hardware do it.

>And your previous swipe " Tie you into their product...." So what's
>wrong with that - perfectly good marketing ploy if you can pull it off -
>but lousy from the consumers' perspective.

Well, if saying that's a "swipe", then count me in as number One "swiper"..!
You must be joking...I can't seriously believe what you are saying. It is
NOT, and never has been, OK for ANY vendor to tie us into their specific
platforms. We can't blame them for trying and if we are silly enough to fall
for it, then we deserve what we get (you know my feelings on this and have
quoted them in threads on this NG...in fact, I seem to recall you desiring
to take a Trans-Atlantic slug at me for being right...(curious concept; I
thought people got hit for being WRONG...<G>))

> Imagine the joy on Fujitsu
>faces if you deleted Chapter 24 from the next re-print of "24 Hours" -
>Fujitsu :) and Bob :(
>
Not having read Thane's book, I have no idea what Chapter 24 is about. But
is bringing joy to people (even Fujitsu...) a BAD thing, now?


>d) And one of the points made by Ray - having gone so far, you can't
>afford to change. Similarly, I'd already committed to OO/GUI before D/S
>became available in N/E
>
"can't afford to change..."?  For my company it was "can't afford NOT to
change..."

>And as regards your comment 'Why..." did he ask. Then suggest you start
>reading some books up on systems analysis. (Frankly I think you've
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>jump in - start with a "Why....?

"I keep six honest serving men
They taught me all I knew
Their names are "What?" and "Where?"
And "When?"
And "How?" and "Why?" and "Who?""

This reflects Kipling's background as a journalist. Whilst we may agree with
his sentiments, we must also accept that by and large, journalists are
pretty disagreeable people... Anyone who has spent time with a two year old,
knows how tiresome constant "Why?"s can become.
It is important to listen to the answers and think about them before
formulating the next "Why?"
So I guess when they told you in the RAF "Get in that plane and kill some
people" and you said "Why?", they figured you might be better suited to
computing..., right, Jimmy<G>?

>I commented to Pete Dashwood that Thane
>said DB was slow. His reply, "They probably don't know the first thing
>about systems design".

Now on this one, Mr Gavan, I really must protest. I have checked the
correspondence, and that is NOT what I said. (I thought it didn't sound like
me and was relieved to find that it wasn't...) Snips in Quotes, my responses
in square brackets...
The first time you raised it:

 "Thane said it was SLOW - his
emphasis, not mine.
[SLOW is always relative. The snail considers the tortoise fast.]"

The second time:
"Thane is still saying under
"Sorting" - that DBs are SLOWwwwww. Care to comment on the NG ?
<<<
[Already have. By now Thane will be sticking pins in a wax effigy of me.]

The comment you may have been thinking of when you "quoted" (again out of
context and NOT verbatim) me, was that when young ACCESS programmers are
thrown in at the deep end "they probably don't know much about DATABASE
design" I did make such a statement.

>And I think their might be a smidgen of truth in
>that. There is a VAST difference between system design and producing
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I heard that the hot-shots stampede to Calgary every year...

>Any compiler/IDE is a compromise; they are conceived as the compiler
>developer views the consumers' needs. As we all know too often they
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
A camel is a horse designed by a committee...

>Nothing to do with Fujitsu or Merant - as I've already indicated to Bob,
>third-party product(s) that address the needs of COBOL users to
…[23 more quoted lines elided]…
>available.

They ARE available already, Jimmy. It's called Active-X, OCX, or CORBA.


>
>And unless some genius dreams up a better way - the only tool I see
>currently to merge this all together is OO with OPEN SOURCE available to
>ALL developers to pick and choose the bits they want.
>

Certainly a possibility but there are already "geniuses" around the planet
who are looking at better ways.

Open Source is irrelevant. OO is another fashion statement (even though I
like the way it fits, it is still just "programming"). Computer Programming
is a phenomenon of the late 20th Century. In fact "developers" will soon be
a thing of the past.

Ultimately, Jo Public will interact with his computer until it does what he
wants. A billion computer users are not going to be dictated to by a
technocracy, neither are they all going to become "computer programmers".
The machines will have to get smart enough for ordinary people to use
easily, and they will. Just as the marketplace drove the user interface on
automobiles to become simple, so will it be with computers.

>Now I've done ranting - but felt somebody had to respond.
>

There, there, Jimmy. Go and lie down in a darkened room...<G>. Hey, it does
no harm to get it off your chest...Much of what you wrote is pertinent. It
certainly came from the heart.

Unfortunately, none of this is getting our friend his toolbar images...

You see what I mean about this NG? Because it is not moderated (at least as
far as I can tell, apologies Mr. Moderator...if you're there.) it is
ESSENTIAL that self discipline be invoked by the contributors. There is a
place for fun and jollity, and "philosophical" discussion, and passing the
time of day, they are all important, but the most important function of this
group is to provide clear support for the COBOL language. That means HELP to
people who need it, if you have the ability to provide it.

For this NG to be effective and actually achieve something, it is necessary
for postings to stay ON-TOPIC. If you want to digress and explore another
avenue, do so by starting another topic.

(As this is one of the exceptional times when I shall post to this NG, I am
not taking my own advice...If I intended to post here regularly, I certainly
would have all of this under another topic...)

All of the above may or may not be valuable, but if anyone wants to refer
back to it he is unlikely to look for it under a topic called "using
Toolbar"....

Look back over this NG and you will find that the most successfull,
sustained threads were the ones which stayed on topic.

Unfortunately, I don't have time to maintain an involvement here (and
besides, there is something hairy with my news server which means I don't
get all the threads anyway) so it doesn't really make any difference to me,
personally, but there may be other people who  would post here if the group
was better organised. (More topics, smaller postings).

If you feel provoked, outraged or incensed by anything I have written, good.
Take it up with me personally. That way I can ensure we stick to the
point...<G>

Regards,

Pete.



I do not intend to participate in this NG because, frankly, it is a waste of
my time. It is a waste of time because threads go off-topic so fast that it
is not possible to follow them. Say, for instance I have particular
expertise which I'm happy to share in the area of "Indexed files". I see a
topic about Indexed files and find that it is now about
>PS: I'll still recommend your book <G>
>
>Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

## Thread: Help needed w/ sequential file — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help needed w/ sequential file

- **From:** Aaron Bassett <abassett2@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3803BEAD.D015D9CB@home.com>`

```
Hi,

    I am working on an assignment for my COBOL class which reads from an
input file, does a few simple calcs, then outputs to a dat file.  I keep
getting extra zeros in my output file and can't seem to pinpoint what
the problem is.  My instructor looked over my code and could not see
anything wrong with it.  He suggested I look over it more closely for
logical errors, but I have already done this for hours.  I'm stumped,
please help.

Excerpt of INPUT file:

00700474R. K. Weaver   3781    0297

00610617Nick Watts     9706    0388

00620769Owen Boswarva  9973    0306

Excerpt of OUTPUT file:

                            FIRST COBOL ASSIGNMENT
                                   AARON BASSETT
STK#      QTY       PRICE       TOTAL
 04      3781       0297        2957
  0         0          0        0000
 06      9706       0388        5928
  0         0          0        0000
 07      9973       0306        1738
  0         0          0        0000
 08      2173       0903        2219
  0         0          0        0000
 09      2735       0193        7855
  0         0          0        0000
 10      3794       0205        7770
  0         0          0        0000
 12      0428       0674        8472
  0         0          0        0000
 13      0200       0904        0800
  0         0          0        0000

CODE (Some of the COMPUTE parts are unfinished but nothing that would
affect the prog):

 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID. ASSIGNMENT-1.
 AUTHOR.  AARON-BASSETT.
 DATE-WRITTEN. 10-5-99.
 DATE-COMPILED.
 SECURITY. GUARD.

 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
 SOURCE-COMPUTER. THIS ONE.
 OBJECT-COMPUTER. THIS ONE.

 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.
  SELECT INPUT-FILE
   ASSIGN TO 'A:\BBC.DAT'
   ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
         SELECT PRINT-FILE
   ASSIGN TO 'A:\PRT.OUT'
   ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.

 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 FD INPUT-FILE
  RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS
  DATA RECORD IS STOCK-IN.
 01 STOCK-IN.
  05 FILLER PIC X(4).
  05 STK-NUM PIC 9(2).
  05 FILLER PIC X(17).
  05 QTY  PIC 9(4).
  05 FILLER PIC 9(4).
  05 PRICE PIC 9(4).
  05 FILLER PIC X(44).

 FD PRINT-FILE
  LABEL RECORDS ARE OMITTED
  RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS
  DATA RECORD IS PRINT-LINE.
 01 PRINT-LINE  PIC X(80).

 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
 01 END-OF-DATA-FLAG PIC X(3) VALUE SPACES.

 01 ASS-LINE.
  05 FILLER PIC X(30) VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(22) VALUE 'FIRST COBOL ASSIGNMENT'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(28) VALUE SPACES.

 01 NAME-LINE.
  05 FILLER PIC X(35)       VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(13) VALUE 'AARON BASSETT'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(32) VALUE SPACES.

 01 HEADING-LINE.
  05 FILLER PIC X(5) VALUE 'STK#'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(3) VALUE 'QTY'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(5) VALUE 'PRICE'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(5) VALUE 'TOTAL'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(43) VALUE SPACES.

 01 DETAIL-LINE.
  05 FILLER PIC X  VALUE SPACES.
  05 DET-STK-NUM PIC 9(2).
  05 FILLER PIC X(6) VALUE SPACES.
  05 DET-QTY PIC 9(4).
  05 FILLER PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  05 DET-PRICE PIC 9(4).
  05 FILLER PIC X(8) VALUE SPACES.
  05 DET-TOTAL PIC 9(4).
  05 FILLER PIC X(44) VALUE SPACES.

 01 DASH-LINE.
  05 FILLER PIC X(29) VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(10) VALUE ALL '-'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(41) VALUE SPACES.

 01 FINAL-TOTAL-LINE.
  05 FILLER PIC X(13) VALUE SPACES.
  05 FILLER PIC X(11) VALUE 'GRAND TOTAL'.
  05 FILLER PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
  05 GRAND-TOTAL PIC 9(6).
  05 FILLER PIC X(43) VALUE SPACES.


 01 STK-TOTAL  PIC 9(4) VALUE ZEROES.

 01 PQ-TOTAL  PIC 9(6) VALUE ZEROES.

 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
 PREPARE-OUTPUT-REPORT.
  OPEN INPUT INPUT-FILE
       OUTPUT PRINT-FILE.
     PERFORM WRITE-HEADING-LINE.
     PERFORM READ-INPUT-FILE.
     PERFORM PROCESS-STK-RECORDS
  UNTIL END-OF-DATA-FLAG = 'YES'.
     PERFORM WRITE-GRAND-TOTAL.
       CLOSE INPUT-FILE
            PRINT-FILE.
       STOP RUN.

 WRITE-HEADING-LINE.
     MOVE ASS-LINE TO PRINT-LINE.
     WRITE PRINT-LINE
  AFTER ADVANCING PAGE.
     MOVE NAME-LINE TO PRINT-LINE.
     WRITE PRINT-LINE.
     MOVE HEADING-LINE TO PRINT-LINE.
     WRITE PRINT-LINE AFTER ADVANCING 1.


 READ-INPUT-FILE.
     READ INPUT-FILE
      AT END MOVE 'YES' TO END-OF-DATA-FLAG
     END-READ.

 PROCESS-STK-RECORDS.
     PERFORM COMPUTE-STK-TOTAL.
     PERFORM WRITE-DETAIL-LINE.
     PERFORM READ-INPUT-FILE.

 COMPUTE-STK-TOTAL.
     COMPUTE STK-TOTAL = PRICE * QTY
     END-COMPUTE.

 WRITE-DETAIL-LINE.
     MOVE STK-NUM TO DET-STK-NUM.
     MOVE QTY TO DET-QTY.
     MOVE PRICE TO DET-PRICE.
     MOVE STK-TOTAL TO DET-TOTAL.
     MOVE DETAIL-LINE TO PRINT-LINE.
     WRITE PRINT-LINE
  AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE.
      *     MOVE SPACES TO PRINT-LINE.
      *     WRITE PRINT-LINE.

 WRITE-GRAND-TOTAL.
     MOVE DASH-LINE TO PRINT-LINE.
     WRITE PRINT-LINE.
     PERFORM COMPUTE-GRAND-TOTAL.
     MOVE PQ-TOTAL TO GRAND-TOTAL.
     MOVE FINAL-TOTAL-LINE TO PRINT-LINE.
     WRITE PRINT-LINE
  AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE.

 COMPUTE-GRAND-TOTAL.
     MOVE STK-TOTAL TO PQ-TOTAL.

Thanks for any help,
Aaron
abassett2@home.com
```

```

wrote:

>Hi,

> 01 DETAIL-LINE.

*reads a record

*your problem is here. you read a record, and there are 2 cr's in the
*input file, not one
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help needed w/ sequential file

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u51lb$lm2@dfw-ixnews16.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<3803BEAD.D015D9CB@home.com> <3806eab1.2865327@nntp.ix.netcom.com> <3805EDD4.FDFBF79A@bigfoot.com>`

```
Phil Brady <delta2@bigfoot.com> wrote in message
news:3805EDD4.FDFBF79A@bigfoot.com...
> >
> > >     PERFORM PROCESS-STK-RECORDS
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I may be mistaken, but I think that the "bad coding practice" was that
END-OF-DATA-FLAG was in the record description (under the FD) rather than in
Working-Storage.  There have been previous discussions (in C.L.C.) about
this, but I think that most (many?) of us prefer having the EOF flag in WS -
rather than in the FD itself (to avoid references to the FD before the 1st
OPEN, or after the last READ or CLOSE).
```

#### ↳ Re: Help needed w/ sequential file

- **From:** AnhMy Tran <anhmytran@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<s0clicq0hu227@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<3803BEAD.D015D9CB@home.com>`

```
1- You should move �ANY� into the EOF-FLAG prior reading.
   Otherwise, the flag may happen to have initial value 'YES'.

2- Your input file STOCK-IN have an extra characters
(81) contradict to the FD (File Definition) that causes
COBOL to read it as a blank record, and inturn, COBOL
generate a blank output record.
In this case, your COBOL compiler does not support the
phrase ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
(Other PC COBOL compiler may do.
In VAX and Mainframe COBOL, organization is always LINE.)
To fix it, define the last FILLER as PIC X(43).

3- May be you have a real blank record in your input file.

4- The Best mothod of Reading is READ INTO a format in
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION. It�s dagerous to read in FD format.

5- The write module can be as followed:
WRITE-DETAIL-LINE.
   IF   LINE-COUNTER  >   50
        PERFORM  WRITE-HEADING-LINE.
   MOVE SPACES        TO  PRINT-LINE.
   MOVE STK-NUM       TO  DET-STK-NUM.
   MOVE QTY           TO  DET-QTY.
   MOVE PRICE         TO  DET-PRICE.
   MOVE STK-TOTAL     TO  DET-TOTAL.
   WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM  DETAIL-LINE AFTER 1.
   ADD  1             TO  LINE-COUNTER.

------------------  Posted via CNET Help.com  ------------------
                      http://www.help.com/
```

<a id="t-444d77c2b2"></a>
## Thread: left alignment — 4 msgs

### left alignment

- **From:** webmaster <webmaster@eii.fr>
- **Date:** 1999-10-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<38031BAB.F9C7D77D@eii.fr>`

```
Hi

I have the following problem :

Working
01 numppid pic s9(9) comp-5 value zeros.

01 ppid    pic z(9).

Proc
call "getppid" returning numppid move
numppid to ppid.

The actual size of the pid is 5 or 6
real digits, I mean 000120253 for
exemple.
My problem is that I need to get the
ppid without blank on the left (I fact I
would need it align to the left). I need
to get : 120253 and not ...120253 (where
the . are blanks in fact).

What can I do ?

thanks

Christophe
```

#### ↳ Re: left alignment

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<XoGM3.31$c%6.582@client>`
- **References:** `<38031BAB.F9C7D77D@eii.fr>`

```
Use INSPECT and reference modification.

webmaster <webmaster@eii.fr> wrote in message
news:38031BAB.F9C7D77D@eii.fr...
> I have the following problem :
> Working
> 01 numppid pic s9(9) comp-5 value zeros.
>
> 01 ppid    pic z(9).
  Change this to pic z(8)9.

   01 leftmost-nonzero pic 99.
   01  target-field  pic X(6).
>
> Proc
> call "getppid" returning numppid move
> numppid to ppid.

   MOVE 1 to leftmost-nonzero.
   INSPECT ppid tallying leftmost-nonzero for leading space.
   MOVE ppid (leftmost-nonzero:) to target-field
>
> The actual size of the pid is 5 or 6
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: left alignment

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<mZGM3.18$Ch2.591@news1.mia>`
- **References:** `<38031BAB.F9C7D77D@eii.fr>`

```
webmaster wrote:
>
>I have the following problem :
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>the . are blanks in fact).

01 numppid pic s9(9) comp-5 value zeros.
01 junknum pic z(9).
01 junktxt pic x(9).
01 ppid    pic x(9).

call "getppid" returning numppid.
move numppid to junknum.
unstring junknum delimited by all spaces into junktxt ppid.
```

#### ↳ Re: left alignment

- **From:** AnhMy Tran <anhmytran@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<s0cljjpihu259@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<38031BAB.F9C7D77D@eii.fr>`

```
Format after calculation, before edit:

01  PPID              PIC Z(9).
01  APID   REDEFINES  PPID.
    05  ONE-SPACES    PIC  X.
    05  APID-ONE      PIC  Z(8). 
01  BPID   REDEFINES  PPID.
    05  TWO-SPACES    PIC  XX.
    05  BPID-TWO      PIC  Z(7). 
01  CPID   REDEFINES  PPID.
    05  THR-SPACES    PIC  XXX.
    05  CPID-THR      PIC  Z(6).

Format after edit:

01  PPID-2            PIC Z(9).
01  APID-2 REDEFINES  PPID.
    05  APID-ONE-2    PIC  Z(8). 
    05  ONE-SPACES-2  PIC  X.
01  BPID-2 REDEFINES  PPID.
    05  BPID-TWO-2    PIC  Z(7). 
    05  TWO-SPACES-2  PIC  XX.
01  CPID-2 REDEFINES  PPID.
    05  CPID-THR-2    PIC  Z(6). 
    05  THR-SPACES-2  PIC  XXX.

and so on  . . .

Put a condition to determin how to Left Alinment:
   IF ONE-SPACES = SPACES
      MOVE  APID-ONE  TO APID-ONE-2
   ELSE
   IF TWO-SPACES = SPACES
      MOVE  BPID-TWO  TO BPID-TWO-2
   ELSE
   IF THR-SPACES = SPACES
      MOVE  CPID-THR  TO CPID-THR-2
   ELSE
      MOVE  PPID      TO PPID-2.

------------------  Posted via CNET Help.com  ------------------
                      http://www.help.com/
```

<a id="t-6bc7752199"></a>
## Thread: Another COBOL "Scheduling" opportunity — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs)_

### Another COBOL "Scheduling" opportunity

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u05rh$v0n$1@nntp5.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
For those in the US (particularly those on the West Coast), all the J4 member
recently received the following note:

"Dear Friend,

My name is Bill Becorest and I am the director of Marketing at Acucorp, Inc.
I am writing to invite you to participate in a national media event that
will provide significant positive exposure for the COBOL programming
language.

The event, tentatively planned for late January 2000, is being coordinated
in conjunction with the US Navy.  It commemorates the significant
achievements of Rear Admiral Grace Murray Hopper and celebrates the 40th
anniversary year of COBOL.  The Navy has given us preliminary agreement for
the use of the USS Hopper for this event.  This active-duty warship is a
multi-mission AEGIS guided missile destroyer that was named in Hopper's
honor.  As you know, former President George Bush awarded Grace Hopper the
National Medal of Technology in 1991, and she is credited with a long list
of accomplishments -- most notably, the development of COBOL.

The commemorative event will be an industry-wide gathering on the USS Hopper
in San Diego, Calif.  A contingent of high-level VIPs from civilian and
military sectors are being invited to attend, including a number of Naval
admirals, California senators Barbara Boxer and Diane Feinstein, San Diego
Mayor Susan Golding, California congressman Randy "Duke" Cunningham,
numerous CEOs and CIOs from major software and hardware corporations,
representatives from major educational institutions, and the US Department
of Commerce.  A national publicity campaign will be launched in November to
attract national attention in print, broadcast and online news outlets.

The purpose of this email is to gauge your interest in either attending the
event yourself or sending a representative.  I will have a preliminary plan
prepared by mid-October, and can forward the details at that time.  Please
feel free to contact me by phone at (858) 689-4430.  I will contact you soon
to request your feedback on this important event.

Thank you.

Bill Becorest
Director of Marketing
ACUCORP, Inc.
858-689-4430    "

   ***

And if you are thinking of arranging your schedule in relationship to this,
you might want to think about including a day or more at the J4 meeting.  (J4
meetings are always "open" and non-binding votes are usually taken of "all
present" including guests.  It is nice/requested, however, that you let J4
know IN ADVANCE, if you plan on visiting.) The January meeting is currently
scheduled for

    17 � 28 January 2000  - (2 weeks Start Monday - end Friday - Sunday off)

The "planned" location WAS Carmel, CA - but there is some talk (now) of
moving it to San Diego (where the already scheduled November meeting is
planned). I wouldn't count on this, but it is possible.

Please do remember that you can see the FULL J4 schedule (at any time) by
visiting the J4 external web page at:

   http://merant.microfocus.com/Standards/
```

#### ↳ Re: Another COBOL "Scheduling" opportunity

- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3804F2DC.B1081809@boeing.com>`
- **References:** `<7u05rh$v0n$1@nntp5.atl.mindspring.net>`

```

the USS Hopper.  It would be kinda crowded if all of us who want o go
actually do.  If the guest list does turn out to be highly restricted, I
am thinking that some of us peons might want to hold something less
restrictive at the same time, and in the same area....?
```

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)

```
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…

Steve:

I'm not sure what Bill has in mind for this, but I just spoke to him a
few minutes ago on the phone and he gave me a very positive impression
that the intent is to have a truly an industry-wide event and not a
platform to promote any individual vendor's product.

If the official ceremony is on the ship and 150,000 COBOL programmers
show up, I certainly agree that it would be tough to get everyone on
board...if that happens, I'll rent a row boat and reserve a seat for
you.  ;-)

Your idea of having other events during the same time and in the same
area is a good one.  In fact, if these other events are scheduled far
enough in advance, it will only serve to draw even more people to the
overall event.

How about...."COBOLfest?"



Bob Wolfe, flexus
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

## Thread: In search of a PL/I - COBOL for OS/390 comparison — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### Re: In search of a PL/I - COBOL for OS/390 comparison

- **From:** R. A. Vowels <robin_v@bigpond.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-12T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.pl1,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6SOM3.3443$_4.6032@newsfeeds.bigpond.com>`

```
"John W. Kennedy" <rri0189@attglobal.net> writes: > robin wrote:
> > There's nothing new in this.  IBM's OS PL/I Version 2 object code was
> > incompatible with Version 1 object code, owing to changes with certain
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> including ours, had problems.

Certain of the builtin functions -- such as HBOUND, LBOUND etc -- were
changed to return FIXED BINARY (31) results (previously FIXED BINARY (15),
necessitating a recompilation.

> -John W. Kennedy
> -rri0189@ibm.net
> Compact is becoming contract
```

## Thread: COBOL and HTML Programming? — 4 this month / 10 total

_Topics: [`Web, XML, modern integration`](topics.md#web)_

### COBOL and HTML Programming?

- **From:** "KansasCityJobs" <kansascityjobs@kc.rr.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<IoSM3.5936$OH2.65938@typhoon2.kc.rr.com>`

```
I work for a large COBOL, CICS mainframe shop.  We are currently developing
a product that will require us to replicate paper statements with HTML
documents.  We have a large number of COBOL programmers and this project
will require us to hire a large number of HTML programmers.  Are there any
products that allow you do generate HTML pages, documents, etc. using COBOL
like commands?  I ran across an email on CobolScript.. is anyone out there
using this for large development projects?

any input is appreciated...

please send resumes to the my email if interested in job opportunities in
the Kansas City area
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL and HTML Programming?

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<38040b38.600214092@news1.attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<IoSM3.5936$OH2.65938@typhoon2.kc.rr.com>`

```
Just get any old HTML editor you like, and place something you can
identify in the fields you want to replace.  Then do a simple read and
write the new file with the fields you want replaced -- well,
replaced.

It need not be complicated.


On Wed, 13 Oct 1999 02:47:04 GMT, "KansasCityJobs"
<kansascityjobs@kc.rr.com> wrote:

>I work for a large COBOL, CICS mainframe shop.  We are currently developing
>a product that will require us to replicate paper statements with HTML
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>

---

```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL and HTML Programming?

- **From:** The COBOL Frog <H.Klink@IMN.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-10-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<38161677.603B6BC7@IMN.nl>`
- **References:** `<IoSM3.5936$OH2.65938@typhoon2.kc.rr.com>`

```
KansasCityJobs wrote:
> 
> I work for a large COBOL, CICS mainframe shop.  We are currently developing
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> the Kansas City area
Accidently, coincidently ...

I work (subcontractor) for a large COBOL CICS mainframe shop, and currently am
expanding a fairly large HTNL front-end system that connects from the web-server
via a communications server with various mainframe CICSes. It uses CICS for NT
for that connection. The COBOL code on the M/F is standard IBM COBOL/CICS. It
includes DB-access (IDMS) and will have SQL (DB/2) in the future.

On the web server the programs (CGI and in the future ISAPI) are COBOL too.
Micro Focus (nowadays called Merant) NetExpress I use for that. It has a builtin
HTML designor/editor and integrated with COBOL fairly well. Some nice JavaScript
could be added if one wants (I do, animating some graphical parts of the html
pages ).

On the web server, these COBOL programs also access elsewhere located network
data bases via ODBC, i.e. standard SQL is also in the web-programs.

The app. is received (used in a inbound call center) with all kinds of
enthousiasm bu the end users. It shows them the information to answer questions
of calling (phoning) customers and intermediates for insurances. This
informationis integrated, browsable and navigatable via hyperlinks and as such
replaces a few hand-full of separate old green-on-blak mainframe screens.
Call/answer time has degraded from 10+ minutes to 2-3 minutes per phone call.
Tremendous savings AND customer satisfaction. 

Can tell you more if needed.

Frog (in a hurry, sorry for typos)
```

### Convert or Port ?

- **From:** "van Daal, Peter" <Peter.vanDaal@nl.origin-it.com>

- **Message-ID:** `<0E16861EE7BCD111BE9400805FE6841F0BE30108@c1s5x001.cor.srvfarm.origin-it.com>`

```
We have a COBOL program, doing communication with the mainframe, that need
also to run on Windows and Unix.
We could :
1) Convert the programs to C or 2) port the cobol programs.
With 1) we need a convert cobol to c package, with 2) we need a strong cobol
compiler (supporting vsam files etc..).

Any suggestions ?

Peter
```

```
(Windows) and IBM COBOLSet (I think that's its current name) and IBM CICS

"simplest" for your particular needs.
```

```
>Thanks for your reply.

From what I have *heard* the VisualAge COBOL does not support the EZA
calls, but you can make the interface making direct calls to the
Windows API - you can go to my site for some examples (In the download
section - sockets.zip) that are geared toward MicroFocus and Fujitsu,
but I think they are sufficiently clear to get you started with VA
COBOL calling the API.
```

```
Peter,

The company that I work for has an EZASOKET implementation for Micro Focus
CICS or Mainframe Express CICS option. It is a tiny part of a bigger
development suite and is not sold as a separate product so it may be less
expensive to write your own EZASOKET interface, especially if you are
comfortable with the WinSock API.  It depends on the value you place on your
time.

More information is available in a reply to your e-mail message. I try to
avoid using the newsgroup for advertising.

Karl
```

```
definitely choice 2. aside from the fact that there are no good cobol
to c converters, if you change to c code, you throw out your
programmers onto the street, AND you have to write in c code from now
on. c is generally a more expensive market, and harder to program in,
and outside of device drivers, compilers and games, you don't want it.

to gvwmoore@spam.ix.netcom.com remove the spam
```

<a id="t-20db3be731"></a>
## Thread: Does COBOL have an INSTR function — 6 msgs

### Does COBOL have an INSTR function

- **From:** trevorwhite@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u1r7h$h4i$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Probably a silly question but how can I find out
if one string contains another -

string 1   "abcde  fghi  jke"
string 2   "cde"

In basic I use instr(string1,string2)

How can I do this in cobol - I've tried inspect,
unsstring but don't seem able to do it - any help
greatfully received.

Thank you
Trevor White


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

- **Message-ID:** `<380476e4_3@news3.prserv.net>`
- **References:** `<7u1r7h$h4i$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```

subroutine at http://etk.com then downloads, then etkpak.
```

#### ↳ Re: Does COBOL have an INSTR function

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8B%M3.663$vk.3667@news3.mia>`
- **References:** `<7u1r7h$h4i$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
trevorwhite@my-deja.com wrote:
>Probably a silly question but how can I find out
>if one string contains another -
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>greatfully received.

The following code is equivalent to BASIC: pos = instr(string1, string2).

There are two easy ways to do what you ask.  If the size of string2 is
constant, you can use this form:

  01  string1       pic  X(99) value "abcde  fghi  jke".
  01  string2       pic  X(03) value "cde".
  01  pos           pic  9(02).
  move 1 to pos.
  inspect string1 tallying pos for characters before string2.

But if string2 is variable size, you can use this form:

  01  string1       pic  X(99) value "abcde  fghi  jke".
  01  string2       pic  X(99).  (*value "cde")
  01  string2-size  pic  9(02).  (*value 3)
  01  pos           pic  9(02).
  move 1 to pos.
  inspect string1 tallying pos for characters before string2(1:string2-size).

* Note: when the value of string2 is set, you must put the actual size
  of the text of string2 into string2-size for the above code to work.

In either case, to test if string2 was found in string1 use:

  if (pos not > function length(string2))
      <string2 is inside string1 at character pos>
```

- **From:** "James King" <mangogwr@bellsouth.net>

- **References:** `<7u1r7h$h4i$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Yes, it is called 'INSPECT' var tallying counter for all | INITIAL | leading
[var | literal].


6.2.21 INSPECT Statement

The INSPECT statement specifies that characters, or groups of characters, in
a data item are to be counted
(tallied) or replaced or both.

   It counts the occurrence of a specific character (alphabetic, numeric,
or special character) in a data item.
    (Formats 1 and 3)

   It fills all or portions of a data item with specified characters, such
as spaces or zeros.  (Formats 2 and 3)

   It converts all occurrences of specific characters in a data item to
user-supplied replacement characters.
    (Format 4)


+--- Format
1 --------------------------------------------------------------------------
-----------+
�
�
�
>>--INSPECT--identifier-1--TALLYING-----------------------------------------
-------------------> �
�
�
�
<---------------------------------------------------------------------------
---------+        �
�
<-------------------------------------------------------------+ �        �
�                                         <------------------+
� �        �
�
>----identifier-2--FOR------CHARACTERS--------------------------------------
------------------>< �
�                           �               +-� phrase 1 +-+
�            �
�                           �
<----------------------------------------+ �            �
�                           �
<------------------+ � �            �
�
+---ALL------------identifier-3---------------------------+            �
�                             +-LEADING-+    +-literal-1----+    +-� phrase
1 +-+                  �
�
�
� phrase 1:
�
�
+----BEFORE-------------------identifier-4----------------------------------
-------------------� �
�    +-AFTER--+  +-INITIAL-+  +-literal-2----+
�
�
�
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-----------------------+
+--- Format
2 --------------------------------------------------------------------------
-----------+
�
�
�
>>--INSPECT--identifier-1--REPLACING----------------------------------------
-------------------> �
�
�
�
<---------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------+         �
�                                         <------------------+
�         �
� >------CHARACTERS
BY----identifier-5----------------------------------------------------------
>< �
�      �                +-literal-3----+    +-� phrase 1 +-+
�           �
�      �
<--------------------------------------------------------------+ �
�
�      �
<------------------+ � �           �
�
+---ALL------------identifier-3----BY----identifier-5-----------------------
----+           �
�        +-LEADING-�    +-literal-1----+      +-literal-3----+    +-� phrase
1 +-+                 �
�        +-FIRST---+
�
�
�
� phrase 1:
�
�
+----BEFORE-------------------identifier-4----------------------------------
-------------------� �
�    +-AFTER--+  +-INITIAL-+  +-literal-2----+
�
�
�
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-----------------------+
+--- Format
3 --------------------------------------------------------------------------
-----------+
�
�
�
>>--INSPECT--identifier-1--TALLYING-----------------------------------------
-------------------> �
�
�
�
<---------------------------------------------------------------------------
---------+        �
�
<-------------------------------------------------------------+ �        �
�                                         <------------------+
� �        �
�
>----identifier-2--FOR------CHARACTERS--------------------------------------
-------------------> �
�                           �               +-� phrase 1 +-+
�            �
�                           �
<----------------------------------------+ �            �
�                           �
<------------------+ � �            �
�
+---ALL------------identifier-3---------------------------+            �
�                             +-LEADING-+    +-literal-1----+    +-� phrase
1 +-+                  �
�
�
�
>--REPLACING----------------------------------------------------------------
-------------------> �
�
�
�
<---------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------+         �
�                                         <------------------+
�         �
� >------CHARACTERS
BY----identifier-5----------------------------------------------------------
>< �
�      �                +-literal-3----+    +-� phrase 1 +-+
�           �
�      �
<--------------------------------------------------------------+ �
�
�      �
<------------------+ � �           �
�
+---ALL------------identifier-3----BY----identifier-5-----------------------
----+           �
�        +-LEADING-�    +-literal-1----+      +-literal-3----+    +-� phrase
1 +-+                 �
�        +-FIRST---+
�
�
�
� phrase 1:
�
�
+----BEFORE-------------------identifier-4----------------------------------
-------------------� �
�    +-AFTER--+  +-INITIAL-+  +-literal-2----+
�
�
�
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-----------------------+
+--- Format 4 -----------------------------------------------------------+
�                                                                        �
� >>--INSPECT--identifier-1--CONVERTING----identifier-6----TO----------> �
�                                        +-literal-4----+                �
�                                                                        �
� >----identifier-7----------------------------------------------------> �
�    +-literal-5----+                                                    �
�                                                                        �
�    <-------------------------------------------+                       �
� >------BEFORE-------------------identifier-4------------------------>< �
�      +-AFTER--+  +-INITIAL-+  +-literal-2----+                         �
�                                                                        �
+------------------------------------------------------------------------+
identifier-1
    Is the inspected item and can be either of the following:

       An alphanumeric data item
       A numeric data item with USAGE DISPLAY
       An external floating point item

Subtopics



6.2.21.2 TALLYING Phrase (Formats 1 and 3)

This phrase counts the occurrence of a specific character (alphabetic,
numeric, or special character) in a
data item.

identifier-2
    Is the count field, and must be an elementary integer item defined
without the symbol P in its PICTURE
    character-string.

    Identifier-2 cannot be:

       A DBCS item
       An external floating point item

    You must initialize identifier-2 before execution of the INSPECT
statement begins.

identifier-3 or literal-1
    Is the tallying field (the item whose occurrences will be tallied).

    Identifier-3 can be any of the following:

       Elementary alphanumeric data item
       Numeric data item with USAGE DISPLAY
       External floating point item

    Literal-1 must be nonnumeric, and can be any figurative constant that
does not begin with the word ALL.
    (If literal-1 is a figurative constant, it is considered to be a
1-character nonnumeric literal.)

CHARACTERS
    When CHARACTERS is specified and neither the BEFORE nor AFTER phrase is
specified, the count field
    (identifier-2) is increased by 1 for each character (including the space
character) in the inspected item
    (identifier-1).  Thus, execution of the INSPECT TALLYING statement
increases the value in the count field
    by the number of characters in the inspected item.

ALL
    When ALL is specified and neither the BEFORE nor AFTER phrase is
specified, the count field (identifier-2)
    is increased by 1 for each non-overlapping occurrence of the tallying
comparand in the inspected item
    (identifier-1), beginning at the leftmost character position and
continuing to the rightmost.

LEADING
    When LEADING is specified and neither the BEFORE nor AFTER phrase is
specified, the count field
    (identifier-2) is increased by 1 for each contiguous non-overlapping
occurrence of the tallying comparand
    in the inspected item (identifier-1), provided that the leftmost such
occurrence is at the point where
    comparison began in the first comparison cycle for which the tallying
comparand is eligible to participate.

FIRST (Format 3 Only)
    When FIRST is specified and neither the BEFORE nor AFTER phrase is
specified, the substitution field
    replaces the leftmost occurrence of the subject field in the inspected
item (identifier-1).
```

#### ↳ Re: Does COBOL have an INSTR function

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<38052496.42886997@news1.attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<7u1r7h$h4i$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
On Wed, 13 Oct 1999 11:42:44 GMT, trevorwhite@my-deja.com wrote:

>Probably a silly question but how can I find out
>if one string contains another -
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Move Zeros to Tally-Counter
Inspect String-1 Tallying Tally-Counter for all String-2

String-2 must be the SIZE of your string.  If string-2 is defined as
Pic X(10) and you search for "CDE" you won't find it.
```

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3805d920.2741485@news.enter.net>`
- **References:** `<VA2N3.991$AX.32430@news1.rdc1.ab.home.com>`

```
"lauone" <lauone@home.com> wrote:

>I need to decompile an exe file made with MF Cobol..ANy such programs out
>there??
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I am aware of only one decompiler from Rasmussen Software, but I think
that it only works with code compiled with an older NCR COBOL
compiler.

I believe Rasmussen has a listing on the flexus tools page.

http://www.flexus.com/tools.html

Please be aware of the fact that even if you can successfully
decompile the code, it may be unmaintainable.  I cannot believe that
any decompiler would have the ability to decompile variable names to
their original text.  The variables will probably be garbled numeric
values which would make the task of maintenance something short of a
nightmare.

This reminds me of the cartoon I once saw of the grieving widow,
dressed in black and standing in front of the freshly dug grave.

A man in a suit behind her was leaning in her direction and asking
her..."Are you *sure* he didn't tell you where he kept the source
code?"



Bob Wolfe, flexus
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

```
There is also a fairly well-known decompiler for IBM mainframe COBOLs
(various releases and products) - from the "Source Recovery Company".
However, this still won't help the person who wanted one for Micro Focus.

HOWEVER, for the person who is trying to "decompile" a Micro Focus .EXE, you
MIGHT be lucky enough to find either a ".INT" or ".IDY" file "hanging around"
with the same name.  If this exists, it might well be possible to recover the
original code.
```

#### ↳ Re: CICS ECI Calls

- **From:** "David Sands" <davs@mfltd.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u50a1$jff$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<#NO1mYcF$GA.168@cpmsnbbsa02>`

```
Here is a snippet of ECI code from a Micro Focus COBOL prog:-

      ***** Populate the CICS ECI Parameter Block

           move low-values to eci-parms
      *
           set  eci-sync            to true
           move "MERANTSR"          to eci-program-name
           display "MERANT"     upon environment-name
           accept eci-system-name   from environment-value
           set  eci-commarea        to address of wl-datahand-commarea
           move length of wl-datahand-commarea
                                    to eci-commarea-length
           move 0                   to eci-timeout
           set  eci-no-extend       to true
           set  eci-version-1       to true

      ***** Perform the synchronous call

           call  cics 'cicsexternalcall'
                 using by reference eci-parms
                 returning ECI-ERROR-ID
           end-call
           perform eci-check-status

You also need a special names

       special-names.
           call-convention 8 is CICS.   *> Used to force static linking

Hope this helps.
David.
```

## Thread: AS/400 COBOL — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`AS/400, iSeries, RPG`](topics.md#as400)_

### AS/400 COBOL

- **From:** "Dennis Griffith" <dgriffit@admin.usf.edu>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u1thh$bt2$1@news.usf.edu>`

```
I have a simple little problem.

I want to move a certain number of bytes to a field with out changing the
rest of the 'to' field.  I must be able to move the bytes to any position on
the 'to' field without altering the rest of the 'to' field.  Normally, I
would use a string command with a  pointer but on the AS/400 there is a 32K
limit on the 'to' field (this limit does not exist on MVS) and this field is
50K.    Any ideas on how I can do this?

Why does the AS/400 COBOL put this limit on the String command?  The from
field does not have it..only the 'to' field....
```

#### ↳ Re: AS/400 COBOL

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<jS%M3.111$sr2.4898@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<7u1thh$bt2$1@news.usf.edu>`

```
01  DESTINATION          PIC X(50K).
01  FILLER REDEFINES DESTINATION.
     05 STUFF   OCCURS 5   PIC X(10K).

MOVE your data to the appropriate offeset and occurrence of STUFF
```

```
MOVE FLD1(start1:length) TO FLD2(start2:length).

I can't remember the format exactly but I'm sure I've used this on the AS400
in both ILE COBOL and I think OPM COBOL.

Rick
```

<a id="t-8898482d10"></a>
## Thread: Borland C & Fujitsu Cobol — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Borland C & Fujitsu Cobol

- **From:** "Nilo" <npaim@zaz.com.br>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u1us0$hqj$1@srv4-poa.zaz.com.br>`

```
Hi folks,

I'm trying to link a Borland C Builder v1 module to a program written in
Fujitsu Cobol v3. The docs on Fujitsu Cobol tell me that the modules i
intend to link must have the following characteristics:

1. model large
2. DS and SS registers discriminated
3. stack check disabled
4. must include windows prologue and epilogue to far functions
5. code generated for 386 instruction set

How could i generate an OBJ that satisfies these characteristics from
Borland C Builder V1? Please note that Fujitsu's documents only mention
MSVC.

If you can help me, many thanks.

If you can't, never mind. I've already sent this question to Borland
and they ignored me. Alias, I've sent it to Fujitsu's tech support too.
They give me the same answer as Borland.

Regards
```

#### ↳ Re: Borland C & Fujitsu Cobol

- **From:** "Cheesle" <cheesle@online.NoSpamPlease.no>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u294m$ade$1@news.cerf.net>`
- **References:** `<7u1us0$hqj$1@srv4-poa.zaz.com.br>`

```
Nilo wrote in message <7u1us0$hqj$1@srv4-poa.zaz.com.br>...
>I'm trying to link a Borland C Builder v1 module to a program written in
>Fujitsu Cobol v3. The docs on Fujitsu Cobol tell me that the modules i
>intend to link must have the following characteristics:

I suggest that you change your C target from obj to be a DLL. DLL's has a
defined interface which is language independent and should not imply
problems.

Cheesle
```

#### ↳ Re: Borland C & Fujitsu Cobol

- **From:** Ed Guy <ed_guy@NOSPAMguysoftware.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-13T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<38049F8B.7DE3@NOSPAMguysoftware.com>`
- **References:** `<7u1us0$hqj$1@srv4-poa.zaz.com.br>`

```
Nilo wrote:
> 
> Hi folks,
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
> They give me the same answer as Borland.

Not sure you'll be able to do this.  The specs you give (e.g. Model Large) imply that it 
expects something from an earlier C or C++ system. Back then you had the ability to 
specify the "memory model" (essentially the addressing scheme and data segment 
locations) as "tiny", "small", "large" and "huge".  I don't see that ability in the 
instructions or help file for C++ Builder and don't know which one it defaults to 
(possibly none of them as it is a 32 bit only system and won't run in a 16 bit system). 

Additionally, it is more like Delphi in nature, but with the event handlers written in 
C++ rather than Pascal.

Probably nobody ever asked either of them your question before and they are still 
thinking about it.
```

<a id="t-64d98a8959"></a>
## Thread: CRC Checksum with Cobol — 2 msgs

### CRC Checksum with Cobol

- **From:** "Brad Feazell" <brad_feazell@dril-quip.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<F01D91744790022D.8CC90BD5354C994F.759266F56A66DB9E@lp.airnews.net>`

```
Does anyone have some Cobol code that will do a CRC checksum on field or
data record?

I realize this is a rather vague question but what I want to do is send
1000 byte records between machines over a socket and make sure that I
receive exactly what has been sent. The machines I'm running on do not
have TCP checksum enabled by default and significant overhead is
incurred by turning it on.
```

#### ↳ Re: CRC Checksum with Cobol

- **From:** "Leif Svalgaard" <lsvalg@ibm.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<38065df6_4@news3.prserv.net>`
- **References:** `<F01D91744790022D.8CC90BD5354C994F.759266F56A66DB9E@lp.airnews.net>`

```

Brad Feazell <brad_feazell@dril-quip.com> wrote in message
news:F01D91744790022D.8CC90BD5354C994F.759266F56A66DB9E@lp.airnews.net...
> Does anyone have some Cobol code that will do a CRC checksum on field or
> data record?
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> incurred by turning it on.

computing the CRC in COBOL also incurs overhead !

The following example is not quite portable. The COMP-4 might have
to be adjusted for your compiler, but the code shows how to
add up characters of the data. For a better CRC you might want
to use a more sophisticated algorithm, but the general principle should
be clear.

000100 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
000200 PROGRAM-ID.    CRC.
000300
000400 AUTHOR.        LEIF SVALGAARD.
000500 DATE-WRITTEN.  99/10/14
000600     -REVISED:  99/10/14.
000700
000800 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
000900
001000 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
001100 SOURCE-COMPUTER. NOT-PORTABLE.
001200 OBJECT-COMPUTER. NOT-PORTABLE.
001300
001400 DATA DIVISION.
001500
001600 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
001700
001800 01  DATA-RECORD.
001900     02  DATA-CHAR               PIC X      OCCURS 80 TIMES.
002000
002100 01  CRC-VARS.
002200     02  CRC-SUM                 PIC S9(18) COMP.
002300     02  CHAR-PTR                PIC S9(3)  COMP.
002400     02  CHAR-VALUE              PIC S9(4)  COMP-4 VALUE +0.
002500     02  FILLER                  REDEFINES  CHAR-VALUE.
002600         03  FILLER              PIC X.
002700         03  THE-CHAR            PIC X.
002800
002900 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
003000     MOVE "SOME DATA" TO DATA-RECORD
003100     MOVE ZEROES TO CRC-SUM
003200     PERFORM FORM-CRC
003300       VARYING CHAR-PTR FROM 1 BY 1
003400         UNTIL CHAR-PTR > 80
003500
003600     DISPLAY CRC-SUM
003700     STOP RUN
003800     .
003900
004000 FORM-CRC.
004100     MOVE DATA-CHAR (CHAR-PTR) TO THE-CHAR
004200     ADD CHAR-VALUE TO CRC-SUM
004300     .
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: moving a alpha numeric field to a numeric field

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aimN3.43018$Ev4.37498@news3.mia>`
- **References:** `<7u4opj$gbs$1@ezekiel.eunet.ie> <AamN3.43009$Ev4.37260@news3.mia>`

```
Judson McClendon wrote:
>Sinead Moore wrote:
>>if i moved a field with of 3 alphanumerics to a 2 digit numeric field
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>can post a message and wait for a response. ;-)

A move to a numeric field is right-justified (or decimal aligned, if
appropriate), and truncated or zero-filled to the left, as applicable.
A move to an alpha (PIC A) or alphanumeric (PIC X) field is left-
justified and space filled or truncated to the right as applicable.

In answer to your question:
  1. The contents of the sending field must be numeric (only 0-9).
  2. The data will be right-justified in the 2 digit numeric field,
     and the leftmost position will be truncated to 2 digits.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: moving a alpha numeric field to a numeric field

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u51qf$lqn@dfw-ixnews16.ix.netcom.com>`

```
Judson McClendon <judmc123@bellsouth.net> wrote in message
news:aimN3.43018$Ev4.37498@news3.mia...
> Judson McClendon wrote:
> >Sinead Moore wrote:
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Judson,
  I think (but haven't checked it out) that a MOVE from a PIC A (not PIC X)
field to a PIC 9 field is "illegal".  It's results are certainly UNDEFINED -
because the former can't include 0-9 and the latter must.
```

## Thread: Move to Last Record — 3 msgs

### Move to Last Record

- **From:** Thanh Q Lam <tlam@newbridge.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3805DA39.8CC5C1B9@newbridge.com>`

```
Does anyone here know how to move to the last record of a found results
using SQL?
I use SQL to query a bath records, now I need some help to move the last
record and store it in a variable.

Thanks in advance,
Thanh
```

#### ↳ Re: Move to Last Record

- **From:** "James King" <mangogwr@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<zgwN3.3637$LW6.58148@news2.mia>`
- **References:** `<3805DA39.8CC5C1B9@newbridge.com>`

```
There is only ONE WAY: loop thru set.

…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
```

#### ↳ Re: Porting source from Win32 to MVS

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u55f6$noq@dfw-ixnews16.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<38057BD7.B772537E@cgi.it>`

```
Coming from a Micro Focus (now MERANT) background, I would suggest that you
look at their products.  However, you should also consider:

  A) IBM's VisualAge (and related products) - this is compatible - because
the "front-end" is actually the identical code.
  B) CA-Realia (definitely targeting the "off-loading" environment)
  C) Fujitsu (I personally think they are more in the Workstation or
Client/Server "target" market than offloading development - but they do have
some IBM mainframe compatibility.  HOWEVER, the last that I checked they did
NOT support the latest IBM mainframe compiler, i.e. IBM COBOL for OS/390 &
VM - with MLE.)
  D) IMHO, RM and AcuCOBOL aren't really in the IBM compatibility business,
but you might still want to check them out.
  E) As far as I know, PerCOBOL totally ignores this market (but would allow
you to develop on the PC and port to a "JAVA-ish" mainframe environment.)
```

#### ↳ Re: Porting source from Win32 to MVS

- **From:** Ed Stevens <ed.stevens@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ohwGOLH=QyfPladNsETLPdROjbLy@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<38057BD7.B772537E@cgi.it>`

```
One approach (and possibly the only one that gives the best chance
source level compatibility) is to not re-host the program in the first
place.  If it is already accessing a data via ODBC, all you need is a
gateway product and appropriate ODBC drive to reach DB2/MVS from the
PC.  With that approach, you shouldn't have to touch the PC pgm (well,
not exactly, as I'll explain in a minute) but simply define a
different ODBC Data Source Name to point through the gateway to DB2.
The pgm will never know the difference --- EXCEPT  . ...  not all
RDBMS's are created equal.  Just has you have vendor extensions to the
COBOL language, you have vendor extensions/interpretations to the
relational data model.  For instance, DB2 has a data type called
"timestamp" which holds time down to the hundredth (thousandth?  I
don't work with it daily, and don't have a manual handy) of a second,
as well as a "date" type and a "time" type.  Most other RDBMS's have
no equivalent to "timestamp" and combines "date" and "time" into a
single data type.  There are also vendor specific
variations/extensions in the SQL language itself.  All this would have
to be accounted for in any porting project.  I would assume that if
you are porting an application pgm from the Windows world to the MVS
world, you are also porting the database, so you would have to
consider these differences in the database design as well as the
application.

Another thing to consider if you decide to actually port the app
itself is that the Win/32 app may very well be doing things for which
there is no equivalent in the MVS world, and therefore no possibility
of 'no intervention' translation.  Things like, perhaps, an API call
to get information from the system registry, or almost any user
interface things.

On Thu, 14 Oct 1999 08:44:39 +0200, Alessandro Santini
<a.santini@cgi.it> wrote:

>Hi everybody,
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>Alessandro

Ed Stevens/TN
```

<a id="t-593f7c9e5c"></a>
## Thread: emptying a table — 4 msgs

### emptying a table

- **From:** "Philippe Lauwers" <philippe.lauwers@securex.be>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u4ic4$544$1@news0.skynet.be>`

```
It's been quite a while since I've been programming in COBOL. I have this
table :

03  WS-A-FKC-FKM                 PIC 9(4) COMP
                                   VALUE 0.
03  WS-TABEL-FKC-FKM             OCCURS 1 TO 1000 TIMES
                                 DEPENDING ON WS-A-FKC-FKM
                                 ASCENDING KEY
                                           IS WS-TABEL-DEP
                                 INDEXED BY WS-INDEX.
    05  WS-TABEL-DEP             PIC X(3).
    05  WS-TABEL-REDEN           PIC X(2).
    05  WS-TABEL-BEGIN           PIC X(8).
    05  WS-TABEL-EINDE           PIC X(8).

that is filled out with a number of elements. I need to initialize it.
WS-A-FKC-FKM needs to be 0 again, but I also want all the elements to be
removed. The results of my program should be exported to a PC-application,
so I want to avoid all LOW-VALUES.

Probably it's not very difficult, but I can't remember how it's done.

Thanks in advance,

Philippe
```

#### ↳ Re: emptying a table

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<LbkN3.2214$LW6.30493@news2.mia>`
- **References:** `<7u4ic4$544$1@news0.skynet.be>`

```
Philippe Lauwers wrote:
>It's been quite a while since I've been programming in COBOL. I have this
>table :
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>that is filled out with a number of elements. I need to initialize it.

MOVE 1000 TO WS-A-FKC-FKM.
INITIALIZE WS-TABEL-FKC-FKM.
MOVE 0 TO WS-A-FKC-FKM.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: emptying a table

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u52mo$mcr@dfw-ixnews16.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7u4ic4$544$1@news0.skynet.be> <LbkN3.2214$LW6.30493@news2.mia>`

```
Judson,
  Today seems to be my day for disagreeing with you <G>  Usually, you are
completely accurate, BUT ...

Being able to INITIALIZE an 01-level above WS-A-FKC-FKM in an extension that
some - but definitely not all compilers allow - because it has a subordinate
OCCURS DEPENDING ON item.  (I don't think anyone would allow an INITIALIZE of
WS-A-FKC-FKM without any index/subscript.) The "portable" way to do this is:

 MOVE 1000 TO WS-A-FKC-FM
 Perform
   Varying WS-Index
     from 1 by 1
     Until WS-Index > 1000
        Initialize WS-TABEL-FKC-FKM (WS-Index)
  End-Perform
  Move Zero to WS-A-FKC-FM

P.S.  Before anyone asks,  "Simply" Moving 0 to WS-A-FKC-FM *may* clear out
all the elements of the table - for future use.  I know of some compilers
that work that way - but the Standard is pretty clear that what will be in
them during the next time WS-A-FKC-FM gets greater than zero is "undefined".

P.P.S.  I think the programmer needs to change
     Occurs 1 to 1000 times
           to
     Occurs 0 to 1000 times

If, OTOH, there IS always at least one element, then the "zeroes" in my
sample need to be changed to 1.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: emptying a table

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<WroN3.43201$Ev4.39379@news3.mia>`
- **References:** `<7u4ic4$544$1@news0.skynet.be> <LbkN3.2214$LW6.30493@news2.mia> <7u52mo$mcr@dfw-ixnews16.ix.netcom.com>`

```
The COBOL 85 standard says in paragraph 6.17.3:

(4) The description of the data item referenced by identifier-1 or any
items subordinate to identifier-1 may not contain the DEPENDING phrase
of the OCCURS clause.

I had forgotten this restriction.  So the table as it stands cannot be
initialized using the COBOL 85 INITIALIZE, my method or yours. :-)

In the case of this particular table, containing only alphanumeric
fields, I would just do this:

MOVE 1000 TO WS-A-FKC-FKM.
MOVE SPACES TO WS-TABEL-FKC-FKM.
MOVE 0 TO WS-A-FKC-FKM.

But to specifically get around the restriction, you could use something
like this:

03  WS-A-FKC-FKM                 PIC 9(4) COMP
                                   VALUE 0.
03  WS-INIT-TABEL-FKC-FKM        OCCURS 1000 TIMES.
    05  WS-INIT-TABEL-DEP        PIC X(3).
    05  WS-INIT-TABEL-REDEN      PIC X(2).
    05  WS-INIT-TABEL-BEGIN      PIC X(8).
    05  WS-INIT-TABEL-EINDE      PIC X(8).
03  WS-TABEL-FKC-FKM             REDEFINES WS-INIT-TABEL-FKC-FKM
                                 OCCURS 1 TO 1000 TIMES
                                 DEPENDING ON WS-A-FKC-FKM
                                 ASCENDING KEY
                                           IS WS-TABEL-DEP
                                 INDEXED BY WS-INDEX.
    05  WS-TABEL-DEP             PIC X(3).
    05  WS-TABEL-REDEN           PIC X(2).
    05  WS-TABEL-BEGIN           PIC X(8).
    05  WS-TABEL-EINDE           PIC X(8).

INITIALIZE WS-INIT-TABEL-FKC-FKM.
MOVE 0 TO WS-A-FKC-FKM.
```

---

<a id="t-f0188cfd65"></a>
## Thread: RMCobol85 5.36 + Windows NT server problems — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### Re: RMCobol85 5.36 + Windows NT server problems

- **From:** Steve Isham <sisham@liant.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3805F59D.7273AF4B@liant.com>`

```
Tony,

There have been several reports of 30,xx or 98,xx errors being returned
by the RM/COBOL for Windows runtime (version 6.51 and greater) when used
with Windows 95, 98, or NT clients connected to Windows NT or Novell
Servers.  The following information, although written to apply to
version of RM/COBOL for 32-bit version of Windows (version 6.5 and
greater), may very well apply to any version of RM/COBOL running 32-bit
Windows.  Keep in mind that RM/COBOL v5.36 has not bee tested with
32-bit Windows.

Generally, the types of status codes that are returned to the COBOL
application are 30 and 98 errors.  Systems experiencing any unusual
frequency of 30 errors, or any 98 errors, should consider making the
changes suggested below.

Liant Software has found that these changes almost always eliminate the
30 or 98 errors.  I strongly recommend that the changes below be applied
to *all* workstations and servers accessing your RM/COBOL programs and
data, even just one workstation or server without these changes can
cause 98,xx and 30,xx errors that can effect all workstations and
servers.

1. VREDIR.VXD
This section applies only to Microsoft Windows 95.
When the Microsoft Client for Microsoft Networks is used, files that
reside on a server (such as a Microsoft Windows NT Server) may become
damaged or may contain invalid data if multiple workstations access the
file at the same time.

This occurs because the network redirector (VREDIR.VXD) caches data
locally for files it accesses on the server. If the last modified time
or file size does not change within a two-second interval, the
redirector reads file data from the local cache rather than from the
actual file on the server.

To resolve this issue, Liant recommends that you ensure that each
Windows 95 machine, has version 4.00.1112 (dated 2/11/97) or later of
the file VREDIR.VXD.

This update can be downloaded from the Microsoft web site:
   http://support.microsoft.com/download/support/mslfiles/vrdrupd.exe

For more information, see the Microsoft Knowledge Base
Article:
   http://support.microsoft.com/support/kb/articles/q148/3/67.asp


2. Network Redirector File Caching
This section applies only to Windows NT Server and Workstation.
By default, when the Windows NT redirector opens a file for read or
read/write access, the redirector using the Windows NT system cache. As
a result, when data is written to the file, it is written to the cache
and not immediately flushed to the redirector. The cache manager flushes
the data at a later time. If an unrecoverable network error occurs while
the data is being transferred to the remote server, it may cause the
write request to fail.

NOTE: Microsoft warns that this change will slow down network I/O.  In
addition, improper registry changes can disable your network server.
Liant Software recommends that only a qualified technician make these
changes to your system.

To disable network redirector file caching, perform the following steps:

A. Start Registry Editor (Regedt32.exe) and go to the following subkey:
                   HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
                     SYSTEM
                       CurrentControlSet
                         Services
                           Rdr
                             Parameters

B. On the Edit menu, click Add Value.

C. Enter the following:
   Value Name: UseWriteBehind ( This entry enables or
                                disables the write-behind
                                optimization for WRITE ONLY
                                files. )
   Data Type:  REG_DWORD
   Data:       0  (1=True, 0=False. The default is 1.)

D. Go to the following subkey:
                   HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
                     SYSTEM
                       CurrentControlSet
                         Services
                           Lanmanworkstation
                             parameters

E. On the Edit menu, click Add Value.

F. Enter the following:
   Value Name: UtilizeNTCaching  ( This entry indicates
                                   whether the redirector
                                   uses the cache manager
                                   to cache the contents
                                   of files. Disable this
                                   parameter to guarantee
                                   that all data is flushed
                                   to the server
                                   immediately after it is
                                   written by the
                                   application. )
   Data Type:  REG_DWORD
   Data:       0  (1=True, 0=False. The default is 1.)

G. Shut down and restart your computer.

For more information, see the Microsoft Knowledge Base Article:
   http://support.microsoft.com/support/kb/articles/q163/4/01.asp


3. Opportunistic Locking on Windows NT
This section applies only to Windows NT Server.

With Opportunistic Locking, if a file is opened in a non-exclusive mode,
the network redirector requests an opportunistic lock of the entire
file. As long as no other process has the file open, the server will
grant this oplock, giving the redirector exclusive access to the
specified file. This will allow the redirector to perform read-ahead,
write-behind, and lock caching, as long as no other process tries to
open the file.

Liant Software recommends that this Windows NT feature be disabled.

You can disable Opportunistic Locking by performing the following steps:

A. Start Registry Editor (Regedt32.exe) and go to the following subkey:
                   HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
                     SYSTEM
                       CurrentControlSet
                         Services
                           LanmanServer
                             Parameters

B. On the Edit menu, click Add Value.

C. Enter the following:
   Value Name: EnableOplocks
   Data Type:  REG_DWORD
   Data:       0  (1=True, 0=False. The default is 1.)

D. Shut down and restart your computer.

For more information, see the Microsoft Knowledge Base Article:
   http://support.microsoft.com/support/kb/articles/q129/2/02.asp


4. Virus Protection Software
This section applies to all Windows and Novell environments.
There have been a few reports of certain virus protection programs
interfering with RM/COBOL data files. This can result in file corruption
or invalid error messages.  If you are experiencing either of these
problems, and have virus protection software either enabled on the
client or the server, Liant Software recommends adjusting the parameters
that control the behavior of the virus protection software.

If possible, you should configure the virus protection software so that
it does not scan the COBOL data files after every modification.  If
necessary, completely disable scanning of COBOL data files.


5. Novell Client32 for Windows 95 Version 2.11 Required
The following paragraph applies to those users who choose to use
Novell's Client32 package for access to NetWare rather than Microsoft's
Client for NetWare.
NOTE: The RM/InfoExpress SPX client, rmspx32.dll, requires the use of
Novell's Client32.
RM/COBOL v6.5 or higher for Windows 95 requires version 2.11 (or later)
of NetWare Client32 for Windows 95 (dated 8/21/96, file size 461,359
bytes).  Record locking does not work properly and file corruption may
occur with earlier versions of Client32.  Additionally there may be open
errors (94,xx) or other permanent errors (30,xx) with older versions of
Client32.  Receiving error 30, MS Windows error 1 (30,12,00001 from
C$RERR) may indicate a defective version of Client32.

Other errors that have been reported are: 30,05 (Access Denied) or
"Error invoking unauthorized copy of runtime on network" error messages.

There have also been reports of NetWare's Client32 for Windows 95
causing file corruption.  Novell recommends turning off "Opportunistic
Locking" and "Packet Burst".  These settings can be adjusted from within
the Control Panel/Network/Client32 Properties/Advanced Settings.

Contact your Novell support representative for further information about
the Client32 software.

I hope this information is helpful to you.

Sincerely,
Steve

== Steve Isham ==-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-==
== Liant Software Corporation    Web Page: www.liant.com  ==
== 8911 Capital of Texas Hwy.    Email: sisham@liant.com  ==
== Austin, Texas 78759 USA                                ==
==-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=--=-==


>Our application is (still) RmCobol 5.36 for DOS and most users are
>running on a Win95 workstation accessing a Netware server.
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>Put a dash instead of dash if emailing me
```

<a id="t-7f7dd6cb1a"></a>
## Thread: The programmer and the frog — 1 msgs

### The programmer and the frog

- **From:** "George E. Zielinski" <georgezielinski@retired.airforce.net>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<17D94448D2333D9F.F3A32DB374F114DB.19713C27BE65B747@lp.airnews.net>`

```
A programmer was crossing a road one day when a frog called out to him and
said, "If you kiss me, I`ll turn into a beautiful princess".

He bent over, picked up the frog and put it in his pocket.

The frog spoke up again and said, "If you kiss me and turn me back into a
beautiful princess, I will stay with you for one week."

The programmer took the frog out of his pocket, smiled at it and returned it
to the pocket.

The frog then cried out, "If you kiss me and turn me back into a princess,
I`ll stay with you and do ANYTHING you want."

Again the programmer took the frog out, smiled at it and put it back into
his pocket.

Finally, the frog asked, "What is the matter?  I`ve told you I`m a beautiful
princess, that I`ll stay with you for a week and do anything you want.  Why
won`t you kiss me?"

The programmer said, "Look I`m a programmer.  I don`t have time for a
girlfriend, but a talking frog, now that`s cool."
```

<a id="t-969f4bc3c7"></a>
## Thread: Don't know how to make a exe file in MFCobol 3.2 — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Don't know how to make a exe file in MFCobol 3.2

- **From:** "Zero" <cyril@hongkong.com>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u2rb6$2ts$1@hfc.pacific.net.hk>`

```
Dear all,
after I wrote a .cbl file, I don't know how to compiler it and a make a .exe
file out. when I am using Micro Focus Cobol  3.2

and I want to know where can I download Micro Focus Personal Cobol
or it menu.
anyone can help me?

Cyril
```

#### ↳ Re: Don't know how to make a exe file in MFCobol 3.2

- **From:** "Ib Tanding" <ib@tanding.dk>
- **Date:** 1999-10-14T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7u509e$ka$1@news.inet.tele.dk>`
- **References:** `<7u2rb6$2ts$1@hfc.pacific.net.hk>`

```
I think that you've reached a point, where the manual comes handy.
You have written a source code, and you must compile it to an object file
(.OBJ), and then you link it. The procedure will vary depending on the
target platform (operating system, Char/GUI etc. etc.)

Look into the sample programs in the demo directory and get some
inspiration.

Visit Merants homepage (merant.com) for information on Micro Focus
compilers.

Regards
Ib
```
